name: [NeuroQuest API] device-signature lockout spike — real installs being rejected

options.escalation_message: Active-install device-signature rejections are STILL above threshold. If you have not already, set DEVICE_AUTH_SOFT_MODE=1 to stop locking members out.

_runbook.why: Validation of device-signature enforcement previously relied on a person manually fetching prod logs and eyeballing them. This monitor automates the wrongful-lockout signal so the documented emergency rollback can be triggered fast.
_runbook.signal: captureMessage('device_signature:<status>', { extra: { active_install } }) in artifacts/api-server/src/lib/deviceAuth.ts. Only missing|invalid|id_mismatch indicate a wrongful lockout; expired (clock skew), issued_at_too_old (rotation), replayed (replay defense) and no_server_key (misconfig) are deliberately EXCLUDED from this monitor.
_runbook.active_install_filter: @extra.active_install:true isolates device-aware clients (sent >=1 device-auth header) from the harmless steady tail of pre-handshake installs that send no headers and always land on `missing`. Without this filter the old-install tail would page.
_runbook.threshold_tuning: Production baseline of active-install rejections is ~0 (post-enforcement prod-log checks found zero non-ok verdicts from active installs). warning=5 / critical=15 over a 15m window tolerates a handful of genuinely broken clients (corrupted keychain, extreme clock skew that slips past the skew window) while paging well before a systemic regression locks out the member base. Revisit if traffic grows materially — at high volume convert to a ratio (active-install rejections / total signed attempts) using the sampled device_signature:ok captures as the denominator.
_runbook.rollback: Set DEVICE_AUTH_SOFT_MODE=1 (no redeploy needed) to return requireDeviceSignature to verify-and-log-without-reject. See .agents/memory/device-signature-enforcement.md.
